derived.mnav_label: deep discount (~0.86× — stock below the value of the Bitcoin it holds)

signal_plain.paragraph: Bitcoin near $63,561, MicroStrategy near $120. The medium-term trend that governs our hedge is firmly negative, so the long-term regime is bearish and defensive positions (shorts / puts) are permitted — we are past 'watch for a top' and into an open hedge. The on-chain mean-reversion gauge is falling toward capitulation and sits just above its capitulation extreme — close to, but not yet in, the deep-value buy zone. No confirmed dip-buy signal, but it is the thing to watch. MicroStrategy trades at a deep discount (~0.86× — stock below the value of the Bitcoin it holds) — a behavioural regime change (below 1.0× the company's own policy inverts toward buying back stock rather than issuing it for Bitcoin).
signal_plain.posture: DEFENSIVE, with a bottom-watch building — trend bearish and hedge open, on-chain stress approaching the buy extreme.

analysts.name: James Check
analysts.summary: On-chain note 'Conviction Test' — says we are at the bear-market stage where 'nobody...absolutely nobody cares about Bitcoin' (he points back to his prior 'Peak Apathy' piece), and flags divergences he says were last seen near the end of the prior bear cycle. That widespread apathy from a top-side voice is textbook capitulation fear — read as weak confirmation of the buy setup per the contrarian-at-extreme rule, not as a real bottom call. His bottom-calling is unreliable (coin flip, prone to fear at lows), so size it small. The note is paywalled; his actual 'updated expectations' for the short and medium term are behind the wall.
analysts.quote: we're at the stage of this bear market where nobody...absolutely nobody cares about Bitcoin

analysts.name: Lyn Alden
analysts.summary: No posts in the window. Lyn is the best all-rounder and a genuine stance-change is high information — her silence here is a real gap on a question (is this a buyable low backed by a liquidity shift?) she is well-suited to answer. Noted honestly, but it leaves the bottom-side without her input.

analysts.name: Michael Howell
analysts.summary: Weekly liquidity update — headline liquidity rose to $193.5tr but 'underlying conditions remain fragile': PBoC tightening faster, Fed liquidity growth fading, dollar strength a drag, Shadow Monetary Base 3m growth still negative. Calls it the 'speculative late-stage of the liquidity cycle' and says the mixed backdrop 'makes risk assets vulnerable to setbacks.' This is a directional restatement of a cautious tide, not a new high-conviction shift, and Howell is weak on timing — so it supports the defensive regime but is not a same-week trigger. He explicitly notes BTC has 'lately lost momentum.' No bottom call.
analysts.quote: This mixed liquidity backdrop makes risk assets vulnerable to setbacks

analysts.name: The Bitcoin Layer
analysts.summary: Two posts. TBL is THE bottom-caller — but its edge only holds while the broader trend is still up, and our trend has decisively broken (open hedge), so discount the buy lean now. Notably it is NOT making a fresh liquidity-pivot bottom call: it reminds readers its indicator flagged a confirmed SELL at $80k (defensive, still in force) and 'Is $60,000 Bitcoin's Bottom?' is largely travelogue plus a message pushing back on fearmongers, with the actual TL;DR/key levels paywalled. The one voice that could add real buy-side conviction did not pull the trigger — that absence matters.
analysts.quote: our TBL Liquidity Indicator flagged a confirmed SELL on May 14th when the bitcoin price was at $80,000

analysts.name: Macro Ops
analysts.summary: Tactical macro — running short BTC/ETH/SOL (the trade 'saved our book'), took partial profits Friday, a new BofA Sell Signal, Liquidity indicator below 50%, Trend Fragility at 76%, and a fresh late-cycle 'Overheating' macro read plus the Alphabet $84.7bn raise as a bearish capex-financing analog. This is a genuine newly-cautious turn off named indicators flipping — exactly when MO is trustworthy on the TOP side. But note: they also flag BTC/ETH 'deeply oversold' at 'significant support' and that ETH was higher 50 days later in 4 of 7 sub-20 daily-RSI cases — a tentative dip-buy lean. Per profile, fade their buy-the-dip; weight the defensive turn.
analysts.quote: increasing conviction... that we're late cycle... I think we're setting up for a larger Summer selloff

analysts.name: Willy Woo
analysts.summary: No posts in the window. Woo is a structural bull whose everyday optimism carries no information; only a rare bearish flip would signal. His silence tells us nothing and is correctly ignored.

bear_overlay.note: Strong TOP-side confluence behind the open hedge. Macro Ops newly turned cautious off named indicators (BofA Sell Signal, Trend Fragility 76%, late-cycle Overheating) and is short BTC/ETH/SOL — a genuine change, MO's trustworthy side. Howell restates a fragile, late-stage liquidity cycle 'vulnerable to setbacks' with BTC momentum fading — directional support, weak on timing. TBL's $80k SELL remains in force. Lyn and Woo silent. Confluence is real but partly restatement, so strength 2 not 3.

qfire.note: BOTTOM-side support is thin and indirect. The on-chain mean-reversion gauge sits just above capitulation but not yet in the buy zone — no confirmed dip-buy. Check's 'absolutely nobody cares' apathy is expert fear at a depressed gauge: per the contrarian-at-extreme rule that is weak CONFIRMATION of the buy setup, not conflict. But the one voice that could truly add buy conviction — The Bitcoin Layer with a liquidity-pivot bottom call — did NOT make one, and its edge is discounted anyway in a broken trend. MO's oversold/RSI note is a fade. Net: the setup is building, corroboration is faint and not yet actionable.

report.system_state: DEFENSIVE — long-term regime bearish, hedge open; on-chain capitulation gauge falling toward but not yet in the deep-value buy zone; no confirmed dip-buy; MSTR at ~0.86x deep discount. Bottom-watch building.
report.bottom_line: SAME conviction. The defensive posture is well-corroborated: Macro Ops genuinely turned cautious and is short crypto, Howell confirms a fragile late-cycle liquidity backdrop, and TBL's $80k sell stands. The bottom-watch is real but not yet confirmed — Check's capitulation-grade apathy is weak buy confirmation, but the one true bottom-caller (TBL) declined to call it and Lyn was silent. Watch for a real TBL liquidity-pivot bottom call and for the on-chain gauge to tag its capitulation extreme; $60k is the level in question.
report.narrative_md:
**Where the system stands.** Bitcoin near $63.6k, MicroStrategy near $120. The medium-term trend that governs our hedge is firmly negative, so the regime is bearish and the hedge is open — we are past watching for a top and into an active defensive stance. The on-chain capitulation gauge is sliding toward its deep-value extreme but hasn't tagged it: no confirmed dip-buy yet, just a bottom-watch worth tracking. MSTR trades at a deep ~0.86x discount to the Bitcoin it holds, the zone where the company's own policy flips toward buying back stock rather than issuing it. Posture: defensive, with a bottom building underneath.

**What the trusted voices said.**
- **Macro Ops (TOP, weight high):** A genuine newly-cautious turn — short BTC/ETH/SOL (the trade 'saved our book'), a fresh BofA Sell Signal, Trend Fragility at 76%, Liquidity below 50%, and a late-cycle 'Overheating' read. This is MO on its trustworthy side, confirming off named indicators flipping. They also flag crypto 'deeply oversold' at support — that dip-buy lean is their weak side; fade it.
- **Michael Howell (TOP, weight medium):** Restates a fragile, 'speculative late-stage' liquidity cycle whose mixed backdrop 'makes risk assets vulnerable to setbacks,' with BTC having 'lately lost momentum.' Directional support for the defensive view, but Howell is early/late on timing — not a same-week trigger.
- **The Bitcoin Layer (BOTTOM, weight low here):** The group's one real bottom-caller, but its edge only holds in an intact uptrend — and ours has broken. Critically, it did NOT make a liquidity-pivot bottom call. It reminds readers its indicator flagged a SELL at $80k and spends 'Is $60,000 Bitcoin's Bottom?' mostly on travel and pushing back on fearmongers; the key levels are paywalled. The trigger that would add buy conviction never fired.
- **James Check (BOTTOM, weight low):** 'Nobody...absolutely nobody cares about Bitcoin' — peak apathy, divergences he says were last seen at the end of the prior bear. Expert fear at a depressed gauge is, by the contrarian-at-extreme rule, weak confirmation of the buy setup, not conflict. But Check is a coin-flip at bottoms; size it small.

**Do the voices confirm the system?** On the defensive side, yes — Macro Ops's real turn plus Howell's fragile-liquidity read line up cleanly behind the open hedge. On the buy side, the support is faint and indirect: the apathy Check describes is what bottoms feel like, but the one voice equipped to call it (TBL) passed, and Lyn — our best all-rounder, the kind of stance-change that would matter — was silent. That silence is a genuine gap, not noise.

**Bottom line.** SAME conviction. The hedge is well-supported; the bottom is forming but unconfirmed. Watch for a real TBL liquidity-pivot bottom call, a Lyn stance-change tied to an actual liquidity shift, and the on-chain gauge tagging its capitulation extreme. $60k is the level in question.

*Coverage note: Lyn Alden and Willy Woo were silent this window.*
